chunks.text: That day, a special exhibition was being held at the museum.
Valuable paintings brought from Europe were being exhibited in Japan for the first time.
At the entrance I bought a ticket and walked along the route.
In the first room, portrait from the fifteenth century were on display.
The faces of kings and nobles, painted with a primitive yet powerful brushstroke.
The next room held impression paintings.
Attempts to capture the light appeared, each with the personality of its artist.
I stopped before one painting and gazed at it for a long time.
Something this painting inspired stayed firmly within me.
A female guide was explaining the historian background of the paintings to newly arrived students.
When I left the museum, I carried a profound satisfaction in my heart.
Art, across time and place, quietly brings relief to the heart of the viewer.
but one of the forms of nutrition that human beings need.
